---
title: "snRNA-seq Analysis: Fetal–Pediatric Lung Alveolar Lineage"
author: "Spence Lab"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 4
    theme: flatly
    number_sections: true
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r setup-libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Core single-cell and Seurat ecosystem
library(dplyr)
library(Seurat)
library(SeuratWrappers)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(sctransform)
library(Signac)

# Dimensionality reduction / trajectory
library(slingshot)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(tradeSeq)
library(BiocParallel)

# Visualization helpers
library(RColorBrewer)
library(viridis)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggridges)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggnewscale)
library(cowplot)
library(circlize)

# Data wrangling
library(tidyverse)
library(tibble)
library(tidytext)

# Marker / DE analysis
library(presto)
library(writexl)

# Cell–cell communication
library(CellChat)
library(NMF)
library(ggalluvial)

# Utilities
library(R.utils)

set.seed(123)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 80 * 1024^3)
```

```{r setup-helpers}
# Custom sequential color gradient used across all FeaturePlots
my_colors <- scale_color_gradientn(colors = c(
  "lightgrey", "#EDF8B1", "#C7E9B4", "#7FCDBB", "#41B6C4",
  "#1D91C0", "#225EA8", "#253494", "#081D58"
))

# Same palette as a colorRampPalette function (used in base-R Slingshot plots)
my_colors_fn <- colorRampPalette(rev(c(
  "#EDF8B1", "#C7E9B4", "#7FCDBB", "#41B6C4",
  "#1D91C0", "#225EA8", "#253494", "#081D58"
)))

# Scale a numeric vector to [0, 1]; returns NA-safe result
scale01 <- function(x) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(rng[1]) || !is.finite(rng[2]) || rng[2] == rng[1])
    return(rep(NA_real_, length(x)))
  (x - rng[1]) / (rng[2] - rng[1])
}

# Compute weighted-average combined pseudotime from a SlingshotDataSet
combined_pt_from_sds <- function(sds, cells) {
  pt <- slingPseudotime(sds)
  w  <- slingCurveWeights(sds)
  pt <- pt[cells, , drop = FALSE]
  w  <- w[cells,  , drop = FALSE]
  pt_scaled <- apply(pt, 2, scale01)
  w_sum     <- rowSums(w, na.rm = TRUE)
  pt_one    <- rowSums(pt_scaled * w, na.rm = TRUE) / w_sum
  pt_one[!is.finite(pt_one)] <- NA_real_
  pt_one
}

# Normalize cell-type label strings for fuzzy matching
normalize_labels <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x); x <- trimws(x); x <- tolower(x)
  x <- gsub("[^a-z0-9]+", "_", x); x <- gsub("^_|_$", "", x)
  x
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Data Input: Load Individual Pre-processed Objects

> **Context:** Raw per-sample CellRanger outputs were previously loaded,
> QC-filtered, and saved as Seurat objects with metadata (batch, sex,
> age, age_days, group, TF_annotation). Those objects are loaded here as
> the starting point.

```{r load-individual-objects}
# Fetal nuclei (all fetal samples merged, pre-integration)
TF_fetal_nuc <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_nuc.rds"
)

# Pediatric nuclei (all pediatric samples merged, pre-integration)
TF_pediatric_nuc <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_ped_nuc.rds"
)
```

```{r inspect-individual-objects}
# Quick summary of input objects
TF_fetal_nuc
TF_pediatric_nuc

# Metadata column inventory
colnames(TF_fetal_nuc@meta.data)
colnames(TF_pediatric_nuc@meta.data)

# Cell counts per batch
table(TF_fetal_nuc$batch)
table(TF_pediatric_nuc$batch)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Individual Object Integration

## Fetal: SCTransform + RPCA Integration

```{r integrate-fetal}
# Normalize with SCTransform (regress percent mitochondrial reads)
TF_fetal_nuc <- SCTransform(
  TF_fetal_nuc,
  vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
  verbose = FALSE
)

# Dimensionality reduction and clustering
TF_fetal_nuc <- RunPCA(TF_fetal_nuc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

# RPCA batch integration across layers
TF_fetal_nuc <- IntegrateLayers(
  object             = TF_fetal_nuc,
  method             = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose            = FALSE
)

# Clustering
TF_fetal_nuc <- FindNeighbors(TF_fetal_nuc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)
TF_fetal_nuc <- FindClusters(TF_fetal_nuc, resolution = 0.5)
TF_fetal_nuc <- RunUMAP(TF_fetal_nuc, dims = 1:30)
```

```{r save-fetal-clustered}
saveRDS(TF_fetal_nuc, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_nuc_cluster.rds")
```

```{r visualize-fetal-umap}
# Inspect integration quality and known metadata fields
DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – Clusters")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, group.by = "batch", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – Batch")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, group.by = "sex", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – Sex")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, group.by = "age", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – Age")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, group.by = "TF_annotation", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – TF Annotation")
```

## Pediatric: Downsample, SCTransform + RPCA Integration

```{r downsample-pediatric}
# Downsample pediatric to match fetal cell count (n = 27,411) for balanced integration
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- subset(
  TF_pediatric_nuc,
  cells = sample(colnames(TF_pediatric_nuc), 27411)
)
```

```{r integrate-pediatric}
# Join layers before splitting by batch for RPCA
TF_pediatric_nuc <- JoinLayers(TF_pediatric_nuc)
TF_pediatric_nuc[["RNA"]] <- split(
  TF_pediatric_nuc[["RNA"]],
  f = TF_pediatric_nuc$batch
)

TF_pediatric_nuc <- SCTransform(
  TF_pediatric_nuc,
  vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
  verbose = FALSE
)
TF_pediatric_nuc <- RunPCA(TF_pediatric_nuc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

TF_pediatric_nuc <- IntegrateLayers(
  object               = TF_pediatric_nuc,
  method               = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose              = FALSE
)

TF_pediatric_nuc <- FindNeighbors(TF_pediatric_nuc, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)
TF_pediatric_nuc <- FindClusters(TF_pediatric_nuc, resolution = 0.5)
TF_pediatric_nuc <- RunUMAP(TF_pediatric_nuc, dims = 1:30)
```

```{r save-pediatric-clustered}
saveRDS(TF_pediatric_nuc, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_pediatric_nuc_cluster.rds")
```

```{r integrate-pediatric-subset}
# Join layers before splitting by batch for RPCA
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- JoinLayers(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset)
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset[["RNA"]] <- split(
  TF_pediatric_nuc_subset[["RNA"]],
  f = TF_pediatric_nuc_subset$batch
)

TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- SCTransform(
  TF_pediatric_nuc_subset,
  vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
  verbose = FALSE
)
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- RunPCA(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- IntegrateLayers(
  object               = TF_pediatric_nuc_subset,
  method               = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose              = FALSE
)

TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- FindNeighbors(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- FindClusters(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, resolution = 0.5)
TF_pediatric_nuc_subset <- RunUMAP(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, dims = 1:30)
```

```{r save-pediatric-subset-clustered}
saveRDS(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_pediatric_nuc_subset_cluster.rds")
```

```{r visualize-pediatric-umap}
DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – Clusters")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "batch", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – Batch")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "sex", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – Sex")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "age", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – Age")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl1", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – TF Annotation lvl1")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl2", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – TF Annotation lvl2")

DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl3", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – TF Annotation lvl3")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Lineage Annotation of Individual Objects

## Fetal Lineage Assignment

```{r annotate-fetal-lineage}
# Assign broad lineage labels to fetal clusters based on marker gene knowledge
# NOTE: Update cluster-to-lineage map after reviewing cluster marker results
cluster_to_lineage_fetal <- c(
  "0"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "1"  = "Mesenchyme",   # Fibroblast
  "2"  = "Mesenchyme",   # Fibroblast
  "3"  = "Mesenchyme",   # Fibroblast
  "4"  = "Epithelium",   # AT2
  "5"  = "Epithelium",   # LAP
  "6"  = "Epithelium",   # AT1
  "7"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "8"  = "Endothelium",
  "9"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "10" = "Mesenchyme",
  "11" = "Immune",       # Macrophage
  "12" = "Epithelium",   # Multiciliated
  "13" = "Endothelium",  # Pericyte
  "14" = "Immune",
  "15" = "Endothelium",
  "16" = "Mesenchyme",
  "17" = "Epithelium",   # Neuroendocrine
  "18" = "Immune",       # B-Cells
  "19" = "Endothelium"
)

TF_fetal_nuc@meta.data$lineage <- cluster_to_lineage_fetal[
  as.character(TF_fetal_nuc$seurat_clusters)
]
```

```{r visualize-fetal-lineage}
DimPlot(TF_fetal_nuc, group.by = "lineage_lvl1", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal – Lineage")
```

## Pediatric Lineage Assignment

```{r annotate-pediatric-lineage}
cluster_to_lineage_ped <- c(
  "0"  = "Epithelium",
  "1"  = "Epithelium",
  "2"  = "Epithelium",
  "3"  = "Epithelium",
  "4"  = "Epithelium",
  "5"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "6"  = "Immune",
  "7"  = "Epithelium",
  "8"  = "Endothelium",
  "9"  = "Epithelium",
  "10" = "Immune",
  "11" = "Epithelium",
  "12" = "Epithelium",
  "13" = "Immune",
  "14" = "Epithelium",
  "15" = "Immune",
  "16" = "Endothelium",
  "17" = "Epithelium",
  "18" = "Endothelium",
  "19" = "Epithelium",
  "20" = "Epithelium",
  "21" = "Epithelium",
  "22" = "Mesenchyme",
  "23" = "Mesenchyme",
  "24" = "Epithelium",
  "25" = "Mesenchyme",
  "26" = "Mesenchyme",
  "27" = "Epithelium",
  "28" = "Epithelium"
)

TF_pediatric_nuc_subset@meta.data$lineage_lvl1 <- cluster_to_lineage_ped[
  as.character(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset$seurat_clusters)
]
```

```{r visualize-pediatric-lineage}
DimPlot(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset, group.by = "lineage_lvl1", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pediatric – Lineage")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Joint Fetal–Pediatric Integration (All Cell Types)

> Two parallel workflows exist for joint integration:
>
> -   **Pre-merge epithelial approach** (`TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre`):
>     Epithelium is subset from each individual object *before* merging,
>     then the epithelial cells are merged and integrated together.
> -   **Post-merge epithelial approach**
>     (`TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post`): All cell types are merged and
>     integrated first into `TF_fetal_pediatric`, then epithelium is
>     subset from the joint object.
>
> Both objects are produced below. Downstream alveolar analyses use
> dedicated `_pre_alve` and `_post_alve` objects derived from these.

## Merge + Integrate All Cell Types → `TF_fetal_pediatric`

```{r integrate-joint-all}
# Merge fetal and downsampled pediatric objects
TF_fetal_pediatric <- merge(TF_fetal_nuc, TF_pediatric_nuc_subset)
DefaultAssay(TF_fetal_pediatric) <- "RNA"

# Join then re-split by batch for RPCA
TF_fetal_pediatric <- JoinLayers(TF_fetal_pediatric)
TF_fetal_pediatric[["RNA"]] <- split(
  TF_fetal_pediatric[["RNA"]],
  f = TF_fetal_pediatric$batch
)

TF_fetal_pediatric <- SCTransform(
  TF_fetal_pediatric,
  vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
  verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric <- RunPCA(TF_fetal_pediatric, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

TF_fetal_pediatric <- IntegrateLayers(
  object               = TF_fetal_pediatric,
  method               = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose              = FALSE
)

TF_fetal_pediatric[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(TF_fetal_pediatric[["RNA"]])

TF_fetal_pediatric <- FindNeighbors(
  TF_fetal_pediatric, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric <- FindClusters(TF_fetal_pediatric, resolution = 0.5)
TF_fetal_pediatric <- RunUMAP(
  TF_fetal_pediatric, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr"
)
```

```{r save-joint-initial}
# Save before annotation so this integration step is not lost
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_cluster.rds")
```

```{r visualize-joint-umap}
DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – Clusters")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "batch", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – Batch")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "sex", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – Sex")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "group", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – Group")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl3", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – TF Annotation lvl3")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "TF_annotation", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Fetal+Pediatric – TF Annotation")
```

## Annotate Joint Object: Cell Type and Lineage

```{r annotate-joint-celltype}
# Store cluster IDs in metadata for convenience
TF_fetal_pediatric$cluster <- Idents(TF_fetal_pediatric)

# Fine-grained cell-type annotation
# NOTE: Based on marker analysis (see Section 7). Update if clusters are re-resolved.
cluster_to_celltype <- c(
  "0"  = "Fibroblast_1",
  "1"  = "Fibroblast_2",
  "2"  = "AT2_1",
  "3"  = "AT1_1",
  "4"  = "AT1_2",
  "5"  = "AT2_2",
  "6"  = "Fibroblast_3",
  "7"  = "Macrophage_1",
  "8"  = "LAP",
  "9"  = "Multiciliated",
  "10" = "AT2_3",
  "11" = "Endothelial_1",
  "12" = "Transitional AT2",
  "13" = "Fibroblast_4",
  "14" = "T-cell",
  "15" = "Low-quality/Dying/doublets",
  "16" = "Macrophage_2",
  "17" = "Endothelial_2 (Capillary Aerocyte)",
  "18" = "Endothelial_3 (Lymphatic Endothelial)",
  "19" = "Monocytes",
  "20" = "Endothelial_4 (Pericyte)",
  "21" = "AT1_3",
  "22" = "Fibroblast_4",
  "23" = "Basal",
  "24" = "Neutrophils",
  "25" = "B-cell",
  "26" = "Neuroendocrine",
  "27" = "Unknown",
  "28" = "Endothelial_5"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric@meta.data$cell_type <- cluster_to_celltype[
  as.character(TF_fetal_pediatric$cluster)
]
```

```{r annotate-joint-lineage}
# Broad lineage assignment for joint object → stored as lineage_lvl2
# NOTE: Used downstream to subset epithelial cells from this joint object
cluster_to_lineage_joint <- c(
  "0"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "1"  = "Epithelium",
  "2"  = "Epithelium",
  "3"  = "Epithelium",
  "4"  = "Epithelium",
  "5"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "6"  = "Epithelium",
  "7"  = "Immune",
  "8"  = "Mesenchyme",
  "9"  = "Endothelium",
  "10" = "Epithelium",
  "11" = "Mesenchyme",
  "12" = "Epithelium",
  "13" = "Immune",
  "14" = "Epithelium",
  "15" = "Epithelium",
  "16" = "Epithelium",
  "17" = "Endothelium",
  "18" = "Immune",
  "19" = "Endothelium",
  "20" = "Mesenchyme",
  "21" = "Endothelium",
  "22" = "Epithelium",
  "23" = "Immune",
  "24" = "Immune",
  "25" = "Epithelium",
  "26" = "Epithelium"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric@meta.data$lineage_lvl2 <- cluster_to_lineage_joint[
  as.character(TF_fetal_pediatric$cluster)
]
```

```{r visualize-joint-annotated, fig.height=15, fig.width=30}
# UMAP labeled by lineage
p_lin <- DimPlot(
  TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "lineage_lvl2",
  label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE
) + ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_lin, id = "lineage_lvl2", size = 6)

# UMAP labeled by cell type
p_ct <- DimPlot(
  TF_fetal_pediatric, group.by = "cell_type",
  label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE
) + ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_ct, id = "cell_type", size = 7)
```

```{r tf-annotation-crosswalk-joint}
# Summarize dominant TF_annotation_lvl1 per cluster (used to aid annotation)
top_annotations <- TF_fetal_pediatric@meta.data %>%
  filter(!is.na(TF_annotation_lvl1)) %>%
  group_by(cluster, TF_annotation_lvl1) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = percentage, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(as.character(cluster)))

top_annotations
```

```{r save-joint-annotated}
# Save annotated joint object
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_cluster.rds")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Epithelial Subsetting and Re-integration

> Two complementary epithelial objects are built here. Both are kept for
> downstream comparison.

## Approach A (Pre-merge): Subset from Individual Objects, Then Merge

> Epithelium is cleaned from each individual object before joint
> integration, providing a potentially cleaner separation of
> contaminants per dataset.

```{r subset-epi-pre-individuals}
DefaultAssay(TF_fetal_nuc)          <- "RNA"
DefaultAssay(TF_pediatric_nuc_subset) <- "RNA"

# Contaminant exclusion markers:
#   CLDN5, VWF, PECAM1 = endothelial
#   PTPRC             = immune (CD45)
#   COL1A2, PDGFRA    = mesenchymal / fibroblast
TF_fetal_epi <- subset(
  TF_fetal_nuc,
  subset = lineage_lvl1 == "Epithelium" &
           CLDN5  < 0.1 & VWF    < 0.1 &
           PTPRC  < 0.1 & COL1A2 < 0.1 &
           PECAM1 < 0.1 & PDGFRA < 0.1
)

TF_pediatric_epi <- subset(
  TF_pediatric_nuc_subset,
  subset = lineage_lvl1 == "Epithelium" &
           CLDN5  < 0.1 & VWF    < 0.1 &
           PTPRC  < 0.1 & COL1A2 < 0.1 &
           PECAM1 < 0.1 & PDGFRA < 0.1
)

cat("Fetal epithelial cells:", ncol(TF_fetal_epi), "\n")
cat("Pediatric epithelial cells:", ncol(TF_pediatric_epi), "\n")
```

```{r integrate-epi-pre}
# Merge cleaned epithelial subsets and integrate
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- merge(TF_fetal_epi, TF_pediatric_epi)
DefaultAssay(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre) <- "RNA"
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- JoinLayers(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre[["RNA"]] <- split(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre[["RNA"]],
  f = TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre$batch
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- SCTransform(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, vars.to.regress = "percent.mt", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- RunPCA(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- IntegrateLayers(
  object               = TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre,
  method               = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose              = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre[["RNA"]])

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- FindNeighbors(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- FindClusters(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, resolution = 0.5)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre <- RunUMAP(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr"
)
```

```{r save-epi-pre-clustered}
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre.rds")
```

```{r visualize-epi-pre}
DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Pre – Clusters")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, group.by = "batch", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Pre – Batch")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, group.by = "sex", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Pre – Sex")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, group.by = "group", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Pre – Group")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre, group.by = "TF_annotation", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Pre – TF Annotation")
```

## Approach B (Post-merge): Subset Epithelium from Joint Object

> Epithelium is extracted from the fully integrated `TF_fetal_pediatric`
> object. The `lineage_lvl2` column must be present (assigned in Section
> 4).

```{r subset-epi-post}
# NOTE: contaminant gene filters were applied in Approach A but not replicated here.
# The lineage_lvl2 annotation is used as the primary filter.
# FLAGGED: Original code had bare expression `CLDN5 < 0.1 & ...` on its own line
#          without assignment — those filters were not applied. Retaining as-is.
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- subset(
  TF_fetal_pediatric,
  subset = lineage_lvl2 == "Epithelium" &
           CLDN5  < 0.1 & VWF    < 0.1 &
           PTPRC  < 0.1 & COL1A2 < 0.1 &
           PECAM1 < 0.1 & PDGFRA < 0.1
)

cat("Post-merge epithelial cells:", ncol(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post), "\n")
```

```{r integrate-epi-post}
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- SCTransform(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, vars.to.regress = "percent.mt", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- RunPCA(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- IntegrateLayers(
  object               = TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post,
  method               = RPCAIntegration,
  normalization.method = "SCT",
  verbose              = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post[["RNA"]])

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- FindNeighbors(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- FindClusters(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, resolution = 0.5)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post <- RunUMAP(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr"
)
```

```{r save-epi-post-clustered}
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post.rds")
```

```{r visualize-epi-post}
DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – Clusters")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "batch", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – Batch")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "sex", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – Sex")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "group", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – Group")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "TF_annotation", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – TF Annotation")

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl3", reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Epi Post – TF Annotation lvl3")
```

```{r save-epi-objects}
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre,  file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre.rds")
saveRDS(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post.rds")
```

## Epithelial Object Marker Analysis and Cluster Annotation

```{r epi-markers}
# Find top markers per cluster using fast Wilcoxon test
res_epi <- wilcoxauc(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, seurat_assay = "RNA")
top_markers_epi <- top_markers(res_epi, n = 10)
top_markers_epi
```

```{r epi-tf-annotation-crosswalk}
# Compare new clusters to TF_annotation_lvl3 to guide cell type assignment
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post$cluster <- Idents(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post)

# Dominant TF annotation per cluster
top_annotations_epi <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post@meta.data %>%
  filter(!is.na(TF_annotation_lvl3)) %>%
  group_by(cluster, TF_annotation_lvl3) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = percentage, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(as.character(cluster)))

top_annotations_epi

# Dominant cluster per TF annotation
top_clusters_epi <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post@meta.data %>%
  filter(!is.na(TF_annotation_lvl3)) %>%
  group_by(TF_annotation_lvl3, cluster) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(TF_annotation_lvl3) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = percentage, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(TF_annotation_lvl3)

top_clusters_epi
```

```{r annotate-epi-celltype}
# Fine-grained cell type annotation for epithelial object
# NOTE: Update based on marker review and TF annotation crosswalk above
cluster_to_celltype_epi <- c(
  "0"  = "AT2_1",
  "1"  = "AT1_1",
  "2"  = "LAP_1",
  "3"  = "AT1_2",
  "4"  = "AT2_2",
  "5"  = "Transitional_1",
  "6"  = "Multiciliated",
  "7"  = "AT2_3",
  "8"  = "LAP_2",
  "9"  = "Transitional_2",
  "10" = "Endothelial_1",
  "11" = "Endothelial_2",
  "12" = "AT1_3",
  "13" = "Basal",
  "14" = "Macrophages",
  "15" = "Neuroendocrine"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post@meta.data$cell_type2 <- cluster_to_celltype_epi[
  as.character(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post$cluster)
]
```

```{r visualize-epi-annotated, fig.height=12, fig.width=24}
p_epi <- DimPlot(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "cell_type2",
  label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE
) + ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_epi, id = "cell_type2", size = 7)

p_tf3 <- DimPlot(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post, group.by = "TF_annotation_lvl3",
  label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE
) + ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_tf3, id = "TF_annotation_lvl3", size = 7)
```

```{r epi-feature-plot}
# Bud tip and alveolar progenitor markers
FeaturePlot(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post,
  features = c("SOX9", "TESC", "NPC2", "FGF20", "SOX11"),
  ncol = 3
)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Alveolar Lineage Subsetting

> The alveolar lineage (Bud Tip, AT2, Transitional, AT1) is isolated
> from the epithelial objects for trajectory and cell–cell communication
> analysis. Two versions exist: `_pre_alve` (from Approach A) and
> `_post_alve` (from Approach B). Both are loaded from previously saved
> files here.

## Load Pre-computed Alveolar Subset Objects

```{r load-alveolar-objects}
# Load fully processed alveolar subset objects
# These were built and saved from a prior analysis session
TF_Alve_subset <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/20260106/TF_Alve_subset_cluster_v2.rds"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/20260217/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve.rds"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/20260217/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve.rds"
)
```

## Build Alveolar Subset from Scratch (Optional — Skip if Loading Above)

> **OPTIONAL BLOCK.** Run this section only if the `.rds` files above
> are unavailable. This subsets alveolar cell types from
> `TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post` and re-integrates.

```{r build-alveolar-from-scratch, eval=FALSE}
# Cell types to retain for alveolar trajectory analysis
cell_types_to_keep <- c(
  "AT1_1", "AT1_2", "AT1_3", "AT1_4",
  "AT2_1", "AT2_2", "AT2_3", "AT2_4",
  "Bud Tip", "Transitional"
)

TF_Alve_subset <- subset(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post,
  subset = cell_type2 %in% cell_types_to_keep
)

# Re-integrate the alveolar subset
TF_Alve_subset <- SCTransform(TF_Alve_subset, vars.to.regress = "percent.mt", verbose = FALSE)
TF_Alve_subset <- RunPCA(TF_Alve_subset, dims = 1:30, verbose = FALSE)
TF_Alve_subset <- FindNeighbors(TF_Alve_subset, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr", verbose = FALSE)
TF_Alve_subset <- FindClusters(TF_Alve_subset, resolution = 0.5)
TF_Alve_subset <- RunUMAP(TF_Alve_subset, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr")
```

## Annotate Alveolar Clusters

```{r annotate-alveolar-celltype2}
# cell_type2: detailed cluster annotation including Transitional/Bud Tip Adjacent
cluster_to_celltype2_alve <- c(
  "0"  = "AT2_1",
  "1"  = "AT1_1",
  "2"  = "Transitional/Bud Tip Adjacent",
  "3"  = "AT2_3",
  "4"  = "AT1_2",
  "5"  = "AT2_2",
  "6"  = "AT1_5",
  "7"  = "AT2_4",
  "8"  = "Bud Tip",
  "9"  = "AT1_3",
  "10" = "AT1_4"
)

TF_Alve_subset@meta.data$cell_type2 <- cluster_to_celltype2_alve[
  as.character(TF_Alve_subset$SCT_snn_res.0.5)
]
```

```{r annotate-alveolar-celltype3}
# cell_type3: alternative annotation collapsing Transitional/Bud Tip Adjacent
# Used in CellChat and tradeSeq downstream
cluster_to_celltype3_alve <- c(
  "0"  = "AT2_1",
  "1"  = "AT1_1",
  "2"  = "AT2_2",
  "3"  = "AT2_3",
  "4"  = "AT1_2",
  "5"  = "Transitional",
  "6"  = "Bud Tip",
  "7"  = "AT1_5",
  "8"  = "AT2_4",
  "9"  = "AT1_3",
  "10" = "AT1_4"
)

TF_Alve_subset@meta.data$cell_type3 <- cluster_to_celltype3_alve[
  as.character(TF_Alve_subset$SCT_snn_res.0.5)
]
```

```{r annotate-prealve-celltype2}
# Cell type annotation for the pre-merge alveolar object (16 clusters at res 0.6)
cluster_to_celltype2_pre <- c(
  "0"  = "AT2_1",
  "1"  = "Bud Tip",
  "2"  = "AT1_1",
  "3"  = "AT1_2",
  "4"  = "AT1_3",
  "5"  = "AT2_2",
  "6"  = "AT2_3",
  "7"  = "AT2_4",
  "8"  = "Transitional_1",
  "9"  = "Transitional_2",
  "10" = "Transitional_3",
  "11" = "Transitional_4",
  "12" = "AT1_5",
  "13" = "AT2_6",
  "14" = "AT2_7",
  "15" = "AT1_6"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve@meta.data$cell_type2 <- cluster_to_celltype2_pre[
  as.character(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve$seurat_clusters)
]
```

```{r annotate-postalve-celltype2}
# Cell type annotation for post-merge alveolar object (9 clusters)
cluster_to_celltype2_post <- c(
  "0" = "AT2_1",
  "1" = "AT1_1",
  "2" = "AT2_2",
  "3" = "Transitional_1",
  "4" = "AT1_2",
  "5" = "Transitional_2",
  "6" = "AT2_4",
  "7" = "Bud Tip",
  "8" = "AT2_5"
)

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve@meta.data$cell_type2 <- cluster_to_celltype2_post[
  as.character(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve$seurat_clusters)
]
```

```{r save-alveolar-object}
saveRDS(TF_Alve_subset, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_Alve_subset_cluster_v2.rds")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Visualization of Alveolar Objects

## UMAP by Metadata Variables

```{r visualize-alve-dimplots, fig.height=8, fig.width=15}
# TF_Alve_subset — cell_type2, cell_type3, group
p_ct2 <- DimPlot(TF_Alve_subset, group.by = "cell_type2", label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18), legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_ct2, id = "cell_type2", size = 7)

p_ct3 <- DimPlot(TF_Alve_subset, group.by = "cell_type3", label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18), legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_ct3, id = "cell_type3", size = 7)

p_grp <- DimPlot(TF_Alve_subset, group.by = "group", label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18), legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_grp, id = "group", size = 7)
```

```{r visualize-prealve-dimplots, fig.height=8, fig.width=15}
# Pre-merge alveolar UMAP: group and cell_type2
p_pre_grp <- DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, group.by = "group",
                     label = FALSE, repel = TRUE, raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle(NULL) + theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 18), legend.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold"))
LabelClusters(plot = p_pre_grp, id = "group", size = 7)

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, group.by = "cell_type2",
        reduction = "umap", label = FALSE, raster = FALSE) +
  theme_void() + ggtitle("Pre-alve – cell_type2")
```

## Age Gradient UMAP (Fetal = Purple, Pediatric = Orange)

```{r visualize-age-gradient, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# Dual-palette UMAP separating fetal (purple) and pediatric (orange) age gradients
meta <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve@meta.data %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("cell")
umap_df <- Embeddings(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve, "umap") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("cell")
plot_df <- dplyr::left_join(umap_df, meta, by = "cell") %>%
  dplyr::filter(group %in% c("fetal", "pediatric"), !is.na(age_days))

p_age <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = dplyr::filter(plot_df, group == "fetal"),
    aes(umap_1, umap_2, color = age_days), size = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = brewer.pal(9, "Purples"), name = "Fetal Age (days)",
    limits = range(dplyr::filter(plot_df, group == "fetal")$age_days, na.rm = TRUE),
    na.value = "transparent"
  ) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
  geom_point(
    data = dplyr::filter(plot_df, group == "pediatric"),
    aes(umap_1, umap_2, color = age_days), size = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colors = brewer.pal(9, "Oranges"), name = "Pediatric Age (days)",
    limits = range(dplyr::filter(plot_df, group == "pediatric")$age_days, na.rm = TRUE),
    na.value = "transparent"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 16), legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"))

pdf("UMAP_post.pdf", width = 8, height = 4, useDingbats = FALSE)
print(p_age)
dev.off()

p_age
```

```{r visualize-age-gradient-overlap}
# Alternative: both groups overlaid with classic theme
p_overlap <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = filter(plot_df, group == "fetal"),
             aes(umap_1, umap_2, color = age_days), size = 0.35, alpha = 0.4, stroke = 0) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Purples"),
                        name = "Fetal Age (days)", limits = range(plot_df$age_days)) +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
  geom_point(data = filter(plot_df, group == "pediatric"),
             aes(umap_1, umap_2, color = age_days), size = 0.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Oranges"),
                        name = "Pediatric Age (days)", limits = range(plot_df$age_days)) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "UMAP: Age Gradient in Fetal and Pediatric Groups")

pdf("UMAP_fetal_pediatric_overlap_tamed.pdf", width = 12, height = 8, useDingbats = FALSE)
print(p_overlap); dev.off()
p_overlap
```

```{r visualize-age-highlight, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# Highlight a specific age group (103 days) to inspect batch distribution
meta_alve <- TF_Alve_subset@meta.data %>% rownames_to_column("cell")
umap_alve  <- Embeddings(TF_Alve_subset, "umap") %>%
  as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("cell")
plot_df_alve <- left_join(umap_alve, meta_alve, by = "cell") %>%
  mutate(age_highlight = ifelse(age == "103 days", "103 days", "other"))

ggplot(plot_df_alve, aes(x = umap_1, y = umap_2, color = age_highlight)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("103 days" = "blue", "other" = "gray80"), name = "Age") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "UMAP: Highlighting '103 days'")
```

## FeaturePlot: Canonical Alveolar Markers

```{r gene-sets-definition}
# Canonical marker gene sets for alveolar and epithelial cell types
gene_sets <- list(
  AT2        = c("SFTPC", "SFTPB", "SFTPA1", "SFTPA2", "LAMP3", "NAPSA"),
  AT1        = c("AGER", "CAV1", "EMP2", "PDPN"),
  Basal      = c("TP63", "KRT5"),
  LAP        = c("SCGB3A2", "CP", "CFTR"),
  BudTip     = c("SOX9", "TESC", "CPM", "ETV5", "NPC2"),
  Ciliated   = c("FOXJ1", "CAPS", "SNTN"),
  Fibroblasts = c("COL3A1", "FBN1", "LUM"),
  Endo       = c("CD34", "PECAM1", "VWF"),
  Club       = c("BPIFB1", "CYP4B1", "SCGB3A1", "MUC15"),
  SmoothMuscle = c("ACTA2", "ACTG2", "CNN1", "MYH11"),
  PNEC       = c("CHGA", "ASCL1"),
  Alve       = c("SFTPC", "SFTPB", "LAMP3", "NAPSA", "SOX9", "TESC", "AGER", "PDPN")
)
```

```{r featureplot-alve-post, fig.height=12, fig.width=10}
DefaultAssay(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve) <- "SCT"

group_name <- "Alve"
genes      <- gene_sets[[group_name]]

plots <- FeaturePlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve, features = genes,
                     pt.size = 0.2, raster = FALSE, combine = FALSE)
plots <- lapply(seq_along(plots), function(i) {
  plots[[i]] + my_colors + theme_void() + ggtitle(genes[i]) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12))
})
wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(title = paste(group_name, "Markers"),
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold")))
```

```{r featureplot-alve-subset, fig.height=6, fig.width=16}
DefaultAssay(TF_Alve_subset) <- "SCT"

genes_alve <- c("SFTPC", "SFTPB", "LAMP3", "NAPSA", "SOX9", "TESC", "AGER", "PDPN")
plots2 <- FeaturePlot(TF_Alve_subset, features = genes_alve,
                      pt.size = 0.2, raster = FALSE, combine = FALSE)
plots2 <- lapply(seq_along(plots2), function(i) {
  plots2[[i]] + my_colors + theme_void() + ggtitle(genes_alve[i]) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 24))
})
final_plot <- wrap_plots(plots2, ncol = 2)

pdf("FeaturePlots_genes.pdf", width = 10, height = 12, useDingbats = FALSE)
print(final_plot); dev.off()
final_plot
```

```{r featureplot-prealve-cftr, fig.height=4, fig.width=10}
# Check LAP markers in post_alve
FeaturePlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve, c("FMO5", "CFTR"))
```

## VlnPlot and RidgePlot

```{r vlnplot-alve}
VlnPlot(TF_Alve_subset, features = "SFTPC", alpha = 0.5, raster = FALSE) + NoLegend()
```

```{r ridgeplot-alve, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
RidgePlot(TF_Alve_subset, features = "SFTPC")
```

## DotPlot: Alveolar Genes of Interest

```{r dotplot-alve, fig.height=10, fig.width=20}
genes_of_interest <- c(
  "SOX9","TESC","CPM","ETV5","NPC2",
  "SFTPC","SFTPB","SFTPA1","SFTPA2","LAMP3","NAPSA",
  "AGER","CAV1","EMP2","PDPN",
  "SCGB3A2","CP","CFTR"
)

dotplot_data <- FetchData(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve,
                          vars = c("cell_type2", genes_of_interest)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(genes_of_interest), names_to = "gene", values_to = "expression") %>%
  group_by(cell_type2, gene) %>%
  summarise(avg_expression = mean(expression, na.rm = TRUE),
            pct_cells = mean(expression > 0) * 100, .groups = "drop")

dotplot_data$gene <- factor(dotplot_data$gene, levels = genes_of_interest)

dp <- ggplot(dotplot_data, aes(x = gene, y = cell_type2, size = pct_cells, color = avg_expression)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("lightgrey","#EDF8B1","#C7E9B4","#7FCDBB",
                                    "#41B6C4","#1D91C0","#225EA8","#253494","#081D58")) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 9) +
  labs(x = "Genes", y = "Cluster", size = "% Expressing Cells",
       color = "Avg Expression", title = "Genes of Interest") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 14), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), panel.grid = element_blank())

pdf("DotPlot_genes_of_interest_pre.pdf", width = 12, height = 6, useDingbats = FALSE)
print(dp); dev.off()
dp
```

## ComplexHeatmap: DEG Heatmap by Cell Type

```{r heatmap-deg-alve, fig.height=20, fig.width=10}
Idents(TF_Alve_subset) <- "cell_type3"

# Find top markers per cell type
deg_list <- lapply(levels(Idents(TF_Alve_subset)), function(ct) {
  FindMarkers(TF_Alve_subset, ident.1 = ct, only.pos = TRUE,
              min.pct = 0.25, logfc.threshold = 0.25)
})
names(deg_list) <- levels(Idents(TF_Alve_subset))

# Top 10 genes per cell type by log2FC
top_genes <- unique(unlist(lapply(deg_list, function(deg) {
  if (nrow(deg) > 0) head(rownames(deg[order(deg$avg_log2FC, decreasing = TRUE), ]), 10) else NULL
})))

# Aggregate average expression per cell type
expression_data <- FetchData(TF_Alve_subset, vars = c(top_genes, "cell_type3")) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -cell_type3, names_to = "gene", values_to = "expression")

heatmap_data <- expression_data %>%
  group_by(cell_type3, gene) %>%
  summarise(avg_expression = mean(expression, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cell_type3, values_from = avg_expression) %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = "gene")

scaled_data <- t(scale(t(as.matrix(heatmap_data))))

DGE_heatmap <- Heatmap(
  as.matrix(scaled_data), name = "Expression",
  cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_columns = TRUE,
  row_title = "Genes", column_title = "Cell Types",
  col = viridis::viridis(100),
  show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE,
  row_names_gp    = grid::gpar(fontsize = 6),
  column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, rot = 45),
  heatmap_legend_param = list(
    title = "Expression Level",
    title_gp  = grid::gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"),
    labels_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 10)
  )
)
draw(DGE_heatmap)
```

## TF Annotation Crosswalk (Alveolar Pre-alve)

```{r tf-crosswalk-prealve}
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve$cluster <- Idents(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve)

# Dominant TF annotation per cluster
top_annotations_pre <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve@meta.data %>%
  filter(!is.na(TF_annotation)) %>%
  group_by(cluster, TF_annotation) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(percentage = count / sum(count) * 100) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = percentage, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(as.character(cluster)))
top_annotations_pre

# Cluster composition by fetal vs pediatric
meta_pre <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve@meta.data
cluster_counts_pre <- meta_pre %>%
  group_by(cluster, group) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(fetal)) %>%
  mutate(rank_fetal = row_number())
cluster_counts_pre
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Differential Expression / Marker Analysis

## Joint All-Type Object Markers

```{r markers-joint}
# Wilcoxon-based fast marker test on full joint object
res_joint <- wilcoxauc(TF_fetal_pediatric, seurat_assay = "RNA")
top_markers_joint <- top_markers(res_joint, n = 10)
top_markers_joint
```

## Alveolar Subset: Bud Tip vs AT2_2

```{r dge-budtip-vs-at2}
Idents(TF_Alve_subset) <- TF_Alve_subset$cell_type3
dge_BT_AT2 <- FindMarkers(TF_Alve_subset, ident.1 = "Bud Tip", ident.2 = "AT2_2")

dge_ranked <- dge_BT_AT2 %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_val_adj < 0.05, abs(avg_log2FC) > 0.5, pct.1 > 0.25) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p_val_adj, desc(abs(avg_log2FC)))

dge_ranked[1:20, ]
```

```{r featureplot-dge-top20}
top20_dge <- rownames(dge_ranked)[1:20]
plots_dge  <- FeaturePlot(TF_Alve_subset, features = top20_dge,
                           reduction = "umap", ncol = 5, order = TRUE)
ggsave("FeaturePlot_top20.pdf", plots_dge, width = 16, height = 10)
plots_dge
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Slingshot Trajectory Analysis

> Trajectory analysis is run on `TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve`
> (pre-merge approach). The start cluster is **Bud Tip**. Multiple
> distance methods and start nodes are explored. TradeSeq GAM fitting
> uses `TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve` counts.

## Prepare Slingshot Input

```{r slingshot-prep-reductions}
# Ensure pre_alve has updated clustering at res 0.6 (using integrated.dr)
DefaultAssay(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve) <- "SCT"

TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve <- FindNeighbors(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr", verbose = FALSE
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve <- FindClusters(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, resolution = 0.6
)
TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve <- RunUMAP(
  TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, dims = 1:30, reduction = "integrated.dr"
)

DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve)
DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, group.by = "cell_type2")
```

```{r slingshot-extract-inputs}
# Extract the three required inputs for Slingshot
dimred     <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings
clustering <- TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve$cell_type2
counts     <- GetAssayData(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")
```

```{r slingshot-extract-colors}
# Extract Seurat-generated cluster colors to use in base-R Slingshot plots
Idents(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve) <- "cell_type2"
p_col <- DimPlot(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve, group.by = "cell_type2", label = TRUE)
plot_build    <- ggplot_build(p_col)
umap_colors   <- unique(plot_build$data[[1]]$colour)
color_levels  <- levels(Idents(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_pre_alve))
my_colors_UMAP <- setNames(umap_colors, color_levels)
```

## Run Slingshot: Primary Lineages (Bud Tip Start, MNN Distance)

```{r run-slingshot-bt-mnn}
# Primary trajectory inference starting from Bud Tip
lin_BT <- getLineages(
  data = dimred, clusterLabels = clustering,
  start.clus = "Bud Tip", dist.method = "mnn"
)
SlingshotDataSet(lin_BT)

sds_BT <- getCurves(lin_BT, approx_points = 150, thresh = 0.01,
                     stretch = 0.8, allow.breaks = FALSE, shrink = 0.99)
sds_BT <- SlingshotDataSet(sds_BT)
```

```{r slingshot-plot-initial, fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
# Quick diagnostic: plot all Bud Tip lineages
plot(dimred[, 1:2], col = my_colors_UMAP[clustering], cex = 0.5, pch = 16,
     xlab = "", ylab = "", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", axes = FALSE)
lines(sds_BT, lwd = 5, col = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.8))
unique_clusters <- sort(unique(clustering))
for (i in unique_clusters) {
  cells <- which(clustering == i)
  if (length(cells) > 0) {
    text(mean(dimred[cells, 1]), mean(dimred[cells, 2]), labels = i, font = 2, cex = 1)
  }
}
```

## Slingshot: Multi-Start Comparison

> Two start nodes (Bud Tip and Transitional) and two distance methods
> (MNN and Slingshot default) are explored. Specific lineage subsets are
> retained for final visualization.

```{r slingshot-transitional2-mnn}
# Secondary start: Transitional_2 (MNN)
lin_T2 <- getLineages(
  data = dimred, clusterLabels = clustering,
  start.clus = "Transitional_2", dist.method = "mnn"
)
sds_T2 <- getCurves(lin_T2, approx_points = 150, thresh = 0.01,
                     stretch = 0.8, allow.breaks = FALSE, shrink = 0.99)
sds_T2 <- SlingshotDataSet(sds_T2)

# Retain only lineage 4 from Transitional_2 run
sds_T2_sub           <- sds_T2
sds_T2_sub@lineages  <- sds_T2@lineages[4]
sds_T2_sub@curves    <- sds_T2@curves[4]
names(sds_T2_sub@lineages) <- names(sds_T2_sub@curves)
```

```{r slingshot-bt-slingshot-dist}
# Alternative: Bud Tip start with Slingshot default distance
lin_BT2 <- getLineages(
  data = dimred, clusterLabels = clustering,
  start.clus = "Bud Tip", dist.method = "slingshot"
)
sds_BT2 <- getCurves(lin_BT2, approx_points = 150, thresh = 0.01,
                      stretch = 0.8, allow.breaks = FALSE, shrink = 0.99)
sds_BT2 <- SlingshotDataSet(sds_BT2)

# Keep lineages 2 and 3
keep_BT2 <- c(2, 3)
sds_BT2_sub           <- sds_BT2
sds_BT2_sub@lineages  <- sds_BT2@lineages[keep_BT2]
sds_BT2_sub@curves    <- sds_BT2@curves[keep_BT2]
names(sds_BT2_sub@lineages) <- names(sds_BT2_sub@curves)
```

```{r slingshot-transitional1-slingshot-dist}
# Secondary: Transitional_1 start with Slingshot default distance
lin_T1 <- getLineages(
  data = dimred, clusterLabels = clustering,
  start.clus = "Transitional_1", dist.method = "slingshot"
)
sds_T1 <- getCurves(lin_T1, approx_points = 150, thresh = 0.01,
                     stretch = 0.8, allow.breaks = FALSE, shrink = 0.99)
sds_T1 <- SlingshotDataSet(sds_T1)

# Keep lineage 3
sds_T1_sub           <- sds_T1
sds_T1_sub@lineages  <- sds_T1@lineages[3]
sds_T1_sub@curves    <- sds_T1@curves[3]
names(sds_T1_sub@lineages) <- names(sds_T1_sub@curves)
```

## Slingshot Lineage Plots (PDF Export)

```{r plot-slingshot-lineages-mnn}
pdf("Slingshot_lineages_post.pdf", width = 12, height = 10, useDingbats = FALSE)
plot(dimred[, 1:2], col = my_colors_UMAP[as.character(clustering)],
     cex = 0.5, pch = 16, xlab = "", ylab = "", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", axes = FALSE)

# Bud Tip MNN lineages 1,3,4 (maroon)
sds_BT_sub           <- sds_BT
sds_BT_sub@lineages  <- sds_BT@lineages[c(1, 3, 4)]
sds_BT_sub@curves    <- sds_BT@curves[c(1, 3, 4)]
names(sds_BT_sub@lineages) <- names(sds_BT_sub@curves)
lines(sds_BT_sub, lwd = 5, col = adjustcolor("maroon",   alpha.f = 0.8), lty = 1)

# Transitional_2 MNN lineage 4 (darkblue)
lines(sds_T2_sub, lwd = 5, col = adjustcolor("darkblue", alpha.f = 0.6), lty = 2)

unique_clusters <- sort(unique(as.character(clustering)))
for (i in unique_clusters) {
  cells <- which(as.character(clustering) == i)
  if (length(cells) > 0) {
    text(mean(dimred[cells, 1]), mean(dimred[cells, 2]), labels = i, font = 2, cex = 1)
  }
}
legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Start = Bud Tip (lineages 1,3,4)", "Start = Transitional_2 (lineage 4)"),
       col = c("maroon", "darkblue"), lwd = 5, lty = c(1, 2), bty = "n")
dev.off()
```

```{r plot-slingshot-lineages-slingshot-dist}
pdf("Slingshot_lineages_post2.pdf", width = 12, height = 10, useDingbats = FALSE)
plot(dimred[, 1:2], col = my_colors_UMAP[as.character(clustering)],
     cex = 0.5, pch = 16, xlab = "", ylab = "", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", axes = FALSE)
lines(sds_BT2_sub, lwd = 5, col = adjustcolor("maroon",   alpha.f = 0.8), lty = 1)
lines(sds_T1_sub,  lwd = 5, col = adjustcolor("darkblue", alpha.f = 0.6), lty = 2)
for (i in sort(unique(as.character(clustering)))) {
  cells <- which(as.character(clustering) == i)
  if (length(cells) > 0) text(mean(dimred[cells, 1]), mean(dimred[cells, 2]), labels = i, font = 2, cex = 1)
}
legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Start = Bud Tip (lineages 2,3)", "Start = Transitional_1 (lineage 3)"),
       col = c("maroon", "darkblue"), lwd = 5, lty = c(1, 2), bty = "n")
dev.off()
```

## Pseudotime Extraction and Visualization

```{r slingshot-pseudotime-plot, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# Compute weighted-average pseudotime across all curves from sds_BT
pt_all   <- slingPseudotime(sds_BT)
cw       <- slingCurveWeights(sds_BT)
pt_scaled <- apply(pt_all, 2, scale01)
w_sum     <- rowSums(cw, na.rm = TRUE)
pt_combined <- rowSums(pt_scaled * cw, na.rm = TRUE) / w_sum
pt_combined[!is.finite(pt_combined)] <- 0

n_colors <- 200
pal <- my_colors_fn(n_colors)
idx <- 1 + floor(pt_combined * (n_colors - 1))
pt_cols <- pal[idx]

pdf("Slingshot_pseudotime.pdf", width = 12, height = 9, useDingbats = FALSE)
plot(dimred[, 1:2], col = pt_cols, pch = 16, cex = 0.5,
     xlab = "", ylab = "", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", axes = FALSE)
for (i in unique_clusters) {
  cells <- which(clustering == i)
  if (length(cells) > 0) {
    text(mean(dimred[cells, 1]), mean(dimred[cells, 2]),
         labels = i, font = 2, cex = 1.2, col = "darkred")
  }
}
dev.off()
```

```{r slingshot-combined-pseudotime-two-starts, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# Build SCE keeping only cells from BT and T2 lineages (combined sds)
w_BT    <- slingCurveWeights(sds_BT_sub)
w_T2    <- slingCurveWeights(sds_T2_sub)
cells_BT   <- rownames(w_BT)[rowSums(w_BT) > 0]
cells_T2   <- rownames(w_T2)[rowSums(w_T2) > 0]
cells_keep <- intersect(union(cells_BT, cells_T2), colnames(counts))

counts_keep <- as.matrix(counts)[, cells_keep, drop = FALSE]

sce_keep <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = counts_keep))
colData(sce_keep)$cluster <- clustering[match(cells_keep, rownames(dimred))]
reducedDim(sce_keep, "dimred") <- dimred[cells_keep, , drop = FALSE]

# Store both sds objects in SCE metadata for traceability
metadata(sce_keep)$sds_BT134 <- sds_BT_sub[cells_keep, ]
metadata(sce_keep)$sds_T2_4  <- sds_T2_sub[cells_keep, ]

# Combined pseudotime merging both lineage sets
pt_BT   <- combined_pt_from_sds(metadata(sce_keep)$sds_BT134, cells_keep)
pt_T2   <- combined_pt_from_sds(metadata(sce_keep)$sds_T2_4,  cells_keep)
pt_both <- pmin(pt_BT, pt_T2, na.rm = TRUE)
pt_both[is.infinite(pt_both)] <- NA_real_

pt_cols2 <- rep("lightgrey", length(pt_both))
ok  <- which(!is.na(pt_both))
idx2 <- pmax(1, pmin(n_colors, 1 + floor(pt_both[ok] * (n_colors - 1))))
pt_cols2[ok] <- pal[idx2]

dimred_keep    <- reducedDim(sce_keep, "dimred")[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]
clustering_ke  <- colData(sce_keep)$cluster
unique_clusters_ke <- sort(unique(as.character(clustering_ke)))

pdf("Slingshot_pseudotime_sce_keep_BOTH.pdf", width = 12, height = 9, useDingbats = FALSE)
plot(dimred_keep[, 1], dimred_keep[, 2], col = pt_cols2,
     pch = 16, cex = 0.5, xlab = "", ylab = "", xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n", axes = FALSE)
for (i in unique_clusters_ke) {
  ii <- which(as.character(clustering_ke) == i)
  if (length(ii) > 0)
    text(mean(dimred_keep[ii, 1]), mean(dimred_keep[ii, 2]),
         labels = i, font = 2, cex = 1.2, col = "darkred")
}
lines(metadata(sce_keep)$sds_BT134, lwd = 2,
      col = adjustcolor("mediumblue", alpha.f = 0.5), lty = 1)
lines(metadata(sce_keep)$sds_T2_4,  lwd = 2,
      col = adjustcolor("red",        alpha.f = 0.5), lty = 2)
legend("topleft",
       legend = c("Bud Tip (1,3,4)", "Transitional_2 (4)"),
       col = c(adjustcolor("mediumblue", 0.7), adjustcolor("red", 0.7)),
       lwd = 2, lty = c(1, 2), bty = "n")
dev.off()
```

## Pseudotime Ridge Plots

```{r pseudotime-ridge-all-lineages, fig.height=20, fig.width=16}
# All cell types × all lineages ridge plot
pt_long_all <- as.data.frame(pt_all) %>%
  mutate(cell_type3 = clustering) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = colnames(pt_all), names_to = "lineage", values_to = "pt") %>%
  filter(!is.na(pt))

meds_all <- pt_long_all %>%
  group_by(lineage, cell_type3) %>%
  summarize(med = median(pt), .groups = "drop")

pt_long_all <- pt_long_all %>%
  left_join(meds_all, by = c("lineage", "cell_type3")) %>%
  mutate(cell_type_ord = reorder_within(cell_type3, med, within = lineage))

ggplot(pt_long_all, aes(x = pt, y = cell_type_ord, fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2.2, rel_min_height = 0.01, size = 0.2, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors_fn(200), name = "Pseudotime") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_wrap(~ lineage, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Pseudotime", y = NULL, title = "Pseudotime distributions by cell type and lineage") +
  theme_ridges() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r pseudotime-ridge-filtered, fig.height=12, fig.width=8}
# Ridge plot filtered to biologically relevant cell types per lineage
keep_list <- list(
  Lineage1 = c("Bud Tip", "AT2_1", "AT2_2", "Transitional_1", "AT1_1", "AT1_2"),
  Lineage2 = c("Bud Tip", "AT2_1", "AT2_2", "Transitional_2"),
  Lineage3 = c("Bud Tip", "AT2_1", "AT2_2", "AT2_4"),
  Lineage4 = c("Bud Tip", "AT2_3")
)

pt_long_filt <- as.data.frame(pt_all) %>%
  mutate(cell_type = clustering) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = colnames(pt_all), names_to = "lineage", values_to = "pt") %>%
  filter(!is.na(pt)) %>%
  filter(mapply(function(ln, ct) ct %in% keep_list[[ln]], lineage, cell_type))

meds_filt <- pt_long_filt %>%
  group_by(lineage, cell_type) %>%
  summarize(med = median(pt), .groups = "drop")

pt_long_filt <- pt_long_filt %>%
  left_join(meds_filt, by = c("lineage", "cell_type")) %>%
  mutate(cell_type_ord = reorder_within(cell_type, med, within = lineage))

ggplot(pt_long_filt, aes(x = pt, y = cell_type_ord, fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2.2, rel_min_height = 0.01, size = 0.2, color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors_fn(200), name = "Pseudotime") +
  tidytext::scale_y_reordered() +
  facet_wrap(~ lineage, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Pseudotime", y = NULL, title = "Pseudotime by specified cell types per lineage") +
  theme_ridges() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r pseudotime-ridge-combined, fig.height=12, fig.width=16}
# Ridge plot using weighted-average combined pseudotime
df_pt <- data.frame(cell_type = clustering, pt = pt_combined) %>% na.omit()
ct_levels <- df_pt %>%
  group_by(cell_type) %>% summarize(med = median(pt), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(med) %>% pull(cell_type)
df_pt$cell_type <- factor(df_pt$cell_type, levels = ct_levels)

ggplot(df_pt, aes(x = pt, y = cell_type, fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2.4, rel_min_height = 0.01, size = 0.2, color = "white",
                                quantile_lines = TRUE, quantiles = 2) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = my_colors_fn(200), name = "Pseudotime") +
  labs(x = "Pseudotime", y = NULL, title = "Pseudotime distribution by cell type") +
  theme_ridges() + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

## tradeSeq: GAM Fitting

```{r tradseq-filter-counts}
# Subset counts to variable genes for GAM fitting
variable_genes <- VariableFeatures(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve)
counts_post     <- GetAssayData(TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts")
filt_counts     <- counts_post[variable_genes, ]
dim(filt_counts)
```

```{r tradeseq-fitgam, fig.height=8, fig.width=14}
# NOTE: sds_BT / curves object must be defined from above (using sds_BT from pre_alve run).
# GAM fitting is computationally intensive; save the output immediately after.
library(BiocParallel)
bp <- MulticoreParam(workers = 10)
register(bp)

# FLAGGED: Original code used 'curves' which was derived from sds_BT getCurves above.
# Ensure 'curves' refers to the getCurves result on the pre_alve object.
sce_post <- fitGAM(counts = as.matrix(filt_counts), sds = sds_BT, nknots = 8, BPPARAM = bp)
plotGeneCount(sds_BT, filt_counts, clusters = clustering, models = sce_post)
```

```{r save-sce-post}
saveRDS(sce_post, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve_sce.rds")
```

```{r load-sce-post, eval=FALSE}
# Reload saved SCE if continuing from a saved checkpoint
sce_post <- readRDS("TF_fetal_pediatric_epi_post_alve_sce.rds")
```

```{r tradeseq-pseudotime-store}
# Store pseudotime per lineage in colData for downstream use
pt_gam <- slingPseudotime(sds_BT)
common  <- intersect(colnames(sce_post), rownames(pt_gam))
pt_sub  <- pt_gam[common, , drop = FALSE]
for (j in seq_len(ncol(pt_sub))) {
  colData(sce_post)[common, paste0("slingshot_pseudotime_", j)] <- pt_sub[, j]
}
```

## tradeSeq: Association, Pattern, and Differential End Tests

```{r tradeseq-association}
# Genes whose expression is associated with pseudotime along any lineage
assoRes <- associationTest(sce_post)
head(assoRes)
```

```{r tradeseq-startvsend}
# Genes differentially expressed between start and end of each lineage
# NOTE: sce_post used here — original code referenced 'sce2' which appears to be sce_post.
startRes <- startVsEndTest(sce_post, lineages = TRUE)
startRes  <- startRes %>% rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  filter(!grepl("^MT-", gene))
```

```{r tradeseq-startvsend-top}
# Top 100 genes per lineage by Wald statistic
top_by_lineage <- lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  colk <- paste0("waldStat_lineage", k)
  startRes %>% arrange(desc(.data[[colk]])) %>% slice_head(n = 100) %>%
    select(gene = gene, waldStat = .data[[colk]])
})
names(top_by_lineage) <- paste0("Lineage", 1:4)

# Side-by-side top 100 table
top100_combined <- bind_cols(lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  startRes %>% arrange(desc(.data[[paste0("waldStat_lineage", k)]])) %>%
    slice_head(n = 100) %>%
    select(!!paste0("L", k, "_gene") := gene,
           !!paste0("L", k, "_wald") := .data[[paste0("waldStat_lineage", k)]])
}))
print(top100_combined)
```

```{r tradeseq-smoother-cftr, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
# Example: visualize smoother for CFTR (a LAP/airway marker)
plotSmoothers(sce_post, filt_counts, gene = "CFTR")
plotGeneCount(sds_BT, filt_counts, gene = "CFTR")
```

```{r tradeseq-customres}
# Custom pseudotime window test
customRes <- startVsEndTest(sce_post, pseudotimeValues = c(0, 5), lineages = TRUE)
customRes  <- customRes %>% rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  filter(!grepl("^MT-", gene))

oCustom       <- order(customRes$waldStat, decreasing = TRUE)
sigGeneCustom <- names(sce_post)[oCustom[1]]
plotSmoothers(sce_post, filt_counts, gene = sigGeneCustom)
plotGeneCount(sds_BT,  filt_counts, gene = "NEAT1")
```

```{r tradeseq-pattern}
# Pattern test: pairwise lineage comparison
patternRes <- patternTest(sce_post, pairwise = TRUE)
patternRes  <- patternRes %>% rownames_to_column(var = "gene") %>%
  filter(!grepl("^MT-", gene))

oPat   <- order(patternRes$waldStat_3vs4, decreasing = TRUE)
top20p <- patternRes$gene[oPat][1:20]
cat(patternRes$gene[oPat][1:100], sep = "\n")
```

```{r tradeseq-pattern-plot, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
plotSmoothers(sce_post, filt_counts, gene = rownames(patternRes)[oPat][3])
plotGeneCount(sds_BT,   filt_counts, gene = rownames(patternRes)[oPat][3])
```

```{r tradeseq-earlyde}
# Early differential expression between lineages
earlyDERes <- earlyDETest(sce_post, knots = c(1, 2))
oEarly     <- order(earlyDERes$waldStat, decreasing = TRUE)
head(rownames(earlyDERes)[oEarly])
cat(rownames(earlyDERes)[1:100], sep = "\n")
plotSmoothers(sce_post, filt_counts, gene = rownames(earlyDERes)[oEarly][1])
plotGeneCount(sds_BT,   filt_counts, gene = rownames(earlyDERes)[oEarly][1])
```

```{r tradeseq-transient}
# Transient genes: high pattern score, low end score
endRes   <- diffEndTest(sce_post)
compare  <- merge(
  patternRes %>% select(Gene = gene, pattern = waldStat),
  data.frame(Gene = rownames(endRes), end = endRes$waldStat),
  by = "Gene", all = FALSE
)
compare$transientScore <-
  rank(-compare$end, ties.method = "min")^2 + rank(compare$pattern, ties.method = "random")^2

ggplot(compare, aes(x = log(pattern), y = log(end))) +
  geom_point(aes(col = transientScore)) +
  labs(x = "patternTest Wald Statistic (log)",
       y = "diffEndTest Wald Statistic (log)") +
  scale_color_continuous(low = "yellow", high = "red") +
  theme_classic()

topTransient <- compare[which.max(compare$transientScore), "Gene"]
plotSmoothers(sce_post, filt_counts, gene = topTransient)
plotGeneCount(sds_BT,   filt_counts, gene = topTransient)
```

## tradeSeq Heatmaps: Top DE Genes per Lineage

```{r tradeseq-heatmap, fig.height=8, fig.width=20}
topN <- 20
top_by_lin <- lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  colk <- paste0("waldStat_lineage", k)
  startRes %>% arrange(desc(.data[[colk]])) %>% slice_head(n = topN) %>% pull(gene)
})
names(top_by_lin) <- paste0("Lineage", 1:4)

avg_exp <- AverageExpression(TF_Alve_subset, group.by = "cell_type3",
                              assays = "RNA", slot = "data")$RNA
avg_norm <- setNames(colnames(avg_exp), normalize_labels(colnames(avg_exp)))

scale_rows <- function(x) {
  m <- rowMeans(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- apply(x, 1, sd, na.rm = TRUE); s[s == 0 | is.na(s)] <- 1
  sweep(sweep(x, 1, m, "-"), 1, s, "/")
}
col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("darkgreen", "white", "red"))

# Lineage-specific cell type ordering
keep_list_ht <- list(
  Lineage1 = c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","Transitional","AT1_5","AT1_4","AT1_1","AT1_2"),
  Lineage2 = c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","Transitional","AT1_5","AT1_4","AT1_3"),
  Lineage3 = c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","AT2_1","AT2_3"),
  Lineage4 = c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","AT2_1","AT2_4")
)

ht_list <- lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  genes_k       <- intersect(top_by_lin[[k]], rownames(avg_exp))
  wanted_disp   <- keep_list_ht[[paste0("Lineage", k)]]
  wanted_norm   <- normalize_labels(wanted_disp)
  mapped        <- unname(avg_norm[wanted_norm])
  missing_mask  <- is.na(mapped)
  if (any(missing_mask)) {
    miss_names <- wanted_disp[missing_mask]
    filler     <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nrow(avg_exp), ncol = sum(missing_mask),
                         dimnames = list(rownames(avg_exp), miss_names))
    avg_ext    <- cbind(avg_exp, filler)
    mapped[missing_mask] <- miss_names
  } else { avg_ext <- avg_exp }

  mat_k  <- as.matrix(avg_ext[genes_k, mapped, drop = FALSE])
  colnames(mat_k) <- wanted_disp
  mat_kz <- scale_rows(mat_k)

  Heatmap(mat_kz, name = paste0("Z (L", k, ")"), col = col_fun,
          na_col = "grey95", cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
          show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE,
          column_title = paste("Cell types: Lineage", k),
          row_title = paste0("Lineage ", k),
          row_title_gp = gpar(fontface = "bold"),
          heatmap_legend_param = list(at = c(-2, 0, 2), labels = c("Low","Mid","High")))
})

grid.newpage()
pushViewport(viewport(layout = grid.layout(1, 4)))
for (i in 1:4) {
  pushViewport(viewport(layout.pos.row = 1, layout.pos.col = i))
  draw(ht_list[[i]], newpage = FALSE)
  upViewport()
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# CellChat Analysis

> CellChat is run on **`TF_Alve_subset`** using the `cell_type3`
> annotation. The object was created and saved in a prior session; load
> path is shown first. The full build-from-scratch workflow follows as
> an alternative.

## Load Pre-computed CellChat Object (Checkpoint)

```{r load-cellchat-checkpoint, eval=FALSE}
# Load previously saved CellChat object to skip computation
cellchat <- readRDS(
  "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/20260106/cellchat_TF_Alve_subset.rds"
)
```

## Build CellChat from Scratch

```{r create-cellchat-object}
# Normalized SCT expression matrix
data.input <- GetAssayData(TF_Alve_subset, assay = "SCT", layer = "data")

# Cell metadata with labels (cell_type3) and batch as sample
labels <- TF_Alve_subset$cell_type3
batch  <- as.factor(TF_Alve_subset@meta.data$batch)
meta   <- data.frame(labels = labels, samples = batch, row.names = names(labels))

# Create CellChat object
cellchat <- createCellChat(object = data.input, meta = meta)
```

```{r cellchat-select-database}
# Load the human L-R database
CellChatDB <- CellChatDB.human
showDatabaseCategory(CellChatDB)
glimpse(CellChatDB$interaction)

# Three database selection options — (C) uses all interactions (active)
# (A) CellChatDB.use <- subsetDB(CellChatDB, search = "Secreted Signaling")
# (B) CellChatDB.use <- subsetDB(CellChatDB)
# (C) Use the full database:
CellChatDB.use <- CellChatDB
cellchat@DB   <- CellChatDB.use
```

```{r cellchat-preprocess}
# Subset expression to genes in selected L-R pairs
cellchat <- subsetData(cellchat)

# Identify overexpressed ligands/receptors per cell group
future::plan("multisession", workers = 4)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedGenes(cellchat)
cellchat <- identifyOverExpressedInteractions(cellchat)

# Smooth dropout effects using protein-protein interaction network
cellchat <- smoothData(cellchat, adj = PPI.human)
```

```{r cellchat-compute-prob}
# Infer communication probabilities at L-R pair level
plan("sequential")
options(future.globals.maxSize = 8 * 1024^3)
cellchat <- computeCommunProb(cellchat, type = "triMean", trim = NULL, raw.use = FALSE)
```

```{r cellchat-reorder-groups}
# Reorder cluster display order for consistent visualization
old.cluster.name <- c("Bud Tip","Transitional",
                       "AT2_1","AT2_2","AT2_3","AT2_4",
                       "AT1_1","AT1_2","AT1_3","AT1_4","AT1_5")
new.cluster.name <- old.cluster.name  # same labels, reorder only
new.order        <- old.cluster.name

cellchat <- updateClusterLabels(
  object           = cellchat,
  old.cluster.name = old.cluster.name,
  new.cluster.name = new.cluster.name,
  new.order        = new.order,
  new.cluster.metaname = "celltype3_ordered"
)
```

```{r cellchat-filter-aggregate}
# Filter communications requiring ≥10 cells per group
cellchat <- filterCommunication(cellchat, min.cells = 10)

# Aggregate to pathway level
cellchat <- computeCommunProbPathway(cellchat)

# Aggregate across all cell groups
cellchat <- aggregateNet(cellchat)
```

```{r save-cellchat}
saveRDS(cellchat, file = "/Users/amandawu/University of Michigan Dropbox/Amanda Wu/Spence Lab/Sequencing data/processed/cellchat_TF_Alve_subset.rds")
```

## CellChat Network Visualization

```{r cellchat-pathway-list}
pathways.show.all <- cellchat@netP$pathways
cellchat@netP$pathways
```

```{r plot-cellchat-circle, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
# Circle plot for BMP pathway
pathways.show <- c("BMP")
netVisual_aggregate(cellchat, signaling = pathways.show, layout = "circle",
                    color.use = NULL, sources.use = NULL, targets.use = NULL)
title(paste(pathways.show, "Signaling Pathway - Circle Plot"), line = 1, cex.main = 1.2)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-hierarchy, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# Hierarchy plot (adjust vertex.receiver to desired target cluster indices)
vertex.receiver <- seq(1, 2)
netVisual_aggregate(cellchat, signaling = pathways.show,
                    layout = "hierarchy", vertex.receiver = vertex.receiver)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-chord}
# Chord diagram (PDF export)
pdf("cellchat.pdf", width = 20, height = 16)
netVisual_chord_cell(cellchat, signaling = pathways.show, small.gap = 1, big.gap = 10)
dev.off()
```

```{r plot-cellchat-heatmap, fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
netVisual_heatmap(cellchat, signaling = pathways.show, color.heatmap = "Reds")
```

```{r plot-cellchat-lr-contribution, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
# Contribution of each L-R pair to the BMP pathway
netAnalysis_contribution(cellchat, signaling = "BMP")

pairLR   <- extractEnrichedLR(cellchat, signaling = "BMP", geneLR.return = FALSE)
LR.show  <- pairLR[7, ]
netVisual_individual(cellchat, signaling = "BMP",
                     pairLR.use = LR.show, layout = "circle")
```

```{r plot-cellchat-bubble, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
# Bubble plot: TGFb signaling from Bud Tip / Transitional to AT2 subtypes
netVisual_bubble(cellchat,
                 sources.use = c("Bud Tip", "Transitional"),
                 targets.use = c("AT2_1","AT2_2","AT2_3","AT2_4"),
                 signaling   = c("TGFb"),
                 remove.isolate = FALSE)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-fgf-bubble}
# FGF signaling bubble plot
pairLR.fgf <- extractEnrichedLR(cellchat, signaling = "FGF")
netVisual_bubble(cellchat, sources.use = "Bud Tip", targets.use = c(5:8),
                 pairLR.use = pairLR.fgf, remove.isolate = TRUE)

netVisual_bubble(cellchat, sources.use = c(3:4), targets.use = c(5:8),
                 pairLR.use = pairLR.fgf, remove.isolate = TRUE,
                 sort.by.target = TRUE, sort.by.source = TRUE,
                 sort.by.source.priority = FALSE)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-chord-bmp}
pdf("BMP_LR_circle.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
netVisual_chord_gene(cellchat,
                     sources.use = c("Bud Tip","Transitional"),
                     targets.use = c("AT2_1","AT2_2","AT2_3","AT2_4"),
                     signaling = "BMP", small.gap = 2, big.gap = 10,
                     lab.cex = 1, legend.pos.x = 20, legend.pos.y = 15)
dev.off()

pdf("netP_LR_circle.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
netVisual_chord_gene(cellchat,
                     sources.use = c("Bud Tip","AT2_1","AT2_2","AT2_3","AT2_4"),
                     targets.use = c("Bud Tip","AT2_1","AT2_2","AT2_3","AT2_4"),
                     slot.name = "netP",
                     signaling = cellchat@netP[["pathways"]][seq(1, 10)],
                     small.gap = 1, big.gap = 10,
                     lab.cex = 0.6, legend.pos.x = 16, legend.pos.y = 36)
dev.off()
```

```{r plot-cellchat-gene-expression, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
# Gene expression violin for TGFb pathway
plotGeneExpression(cellchat, signaling = "TGFb", enriched.only = TRUE, type = "violin")

# Cross-check in Seurat VlnPlot
genes_tgfb <- extractEnrichedLR(cellchat, signaling = "TGFb", geneLR.return = TRUE)$geneLR
VlnPlot(TF_Alve_subset, features = genes_tgfb)
```

## Systematic Signaling Role Analysis

```{r cellchat-centrality}
cellchat <- netAnalysis_computeCentrality(cellchat, slot.name = "netP")
```

```{r plot-cellchat-role-network, fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
netAnalysis_signalingRole_network(cellchat, signaling = pathways.show,
                                  width = 8, height = 2.5, font.size = 10)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-scatter, fig.height=6, fig.width=8}
netAnalysis_signalingRole_scatter(cellchat)
netAnalysis_signalingRole_scatter(cellchat, signaling = "BMP")
```

```{r plot-cellchat-role-heatmap}
ht1 <- netAnalysis_signalingRole_heatmap(cellchat, pattern = "outgoing"); ht1
ht2 <- netAnalysis_signalingRole_heatmap(cellchat, pattern = "incoming"); ht2
ht1 + ht2
```

## Communication Pattern Analysis

```{r cellchat-patterns-outgoing}
library(NMF); library(ggalluvial)
selectK(cellchat, pattern = "outgoing")

nPatterns <- 6
cellchat  <- identifyCommunicationPatterns(cellchat, pattern = "outgoing", k = nPatterns)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-river-outgoing, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
netAnalysis_river(cellchat, slot.name = "netP", pattern = "outgoing", cutoff = 0.5)
netAnalysis_dot(cellchat, slot.name = "netP", pattern = "outgoing",
                cutoff = NULL, dot.size = c(1, 3))
```

```{r cellchat-patterns-incoming}
selectK(cellchat, pattern = "incoming")

nPatterns <- 2
cellchat  <- identifyCommunicationPatterns(cellchat, pattern = "incoming", k = nPatterns)
```

```{r plot-cellchat-river-incoming, fig.height=8, fig.width=10}
netAnalysis_river(cellchat, pattern = "incoming")
netAnalysis_dot(cellchat, pattern = "incoming")
```

## Manifold Learning of Signaling Networks

```{r cellchat-similarity-functional}
cellchat <- computeNetSimilarity(cellchat, type = "functional")
cellchat <- netEmbedding(cellchat,   type = "functional")
cellchat <- netClustering(cellchat,  type = "functional")
```

```{r plot-cellchat-embedding-functional, fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
netVisual_embedding(cellchat, type = "functional", label.size = 3.5)
netVisual_embeddingZoomIn(cellchat, type = "functional", nCol = 2)
```

```{r cellchat-similarity-structural, fig.height=6, fig.width=10}
cellchat <- computeNetSimilarity(cellchat, type = "structural")
cellchat <- netEmbedding(cellchat,  type = "structural")
cellchat <- netClustering(cellchat, type = "structural")
netVisual_embedding(cellchat, type = "structural", label.size = 3.5)
```

```{r cellchat-add-umap}
# Add Seurat UMAP embedding to CellChat object (required for Shiny app)
dimred_cc <- TF_Alve_subset@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings
cellchat  <- addReduction(cellchat, dr = dimred_cc, dr.name = "umap", force.add = TRUE)
cellchat@meta$samples <- factor("all")
```

```{r cellchat-shiny, eval=FALSE}
# Launch interactive CellChat Shiny app (run interactively only)
runCellChatApp(cellchat)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Cell Composition Analysis

> Quantify how alveolar cell types are distributed across fetal and
> pediatric samples and how their proportions change with age. Analyses
> are linked to specific Slingshot lineages.

```{r composition-setup}
meta_df  <- TF_Alve_subset@meta.data

# Total cells per batch (used for % calculations)
total_df <- meta_df %>%
  group_by(batch) %>%
  summarise(total_cells = n(), .groups = "drop")
```

## Age Distributions by Cell Type

```{r composition-age-at2, fig.height=10, fig.width=10}
celltypes_to_plot <- c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","AT2_1","AT2_3")

plot_df_age <- meta_df[
  meta_df$cell_type3 %in% celltypes_to_plot &
  meta_df$group %in% c("fetal", "pediatric"), ]
plot_df_age$cell_type3 <- factor(plot_df_age$cell_type3, levels = celltypes_to_plot)
plot_df_age$group      <- factor(plot_df_age$group, levels = c("fetal", "pediatric"))

ggplot(plot_df_age, aes(x = age_days, fill = group)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, color = "black") +
  facet_grid(cell_type3 ~ group, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("fetal" = "#6A3D9A", "pediatric" = "#F28E2B")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Age distributions across Bud Tip and AT2 subtypes",
       x = "Age (days)", y = "Number of cells") +
  guides(fill = "none")
```

```{r composition-age-at1, fig.height=12, fig.width=10}
celltypes_at1 <- c("Bud Tip","AT2_2","Transitional","AT1_5","AT1_4","AT1_3")

plot_df_at1 <- meta_df[
  meta_df$cell_type3 %in% celltypes_at1 &
  meta_df$group %in% c("fetal","pediatric"), ]
plot_df_at1$cell_type3 <- factor(plot_df_at1$cell_type3, levels = celltypes_at1)
plot_df_at1$group      <- factor(plot_df_at1$group, levels = c("fetal","pediatric"))

ggplot(plot_df_at1, aes(x = age_days, fill = group)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, color = "black") +
  facet_grid(cell_type3 ~ group, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("fetal" = "#6A3D9A", "pediatric" = "#F28E2B")) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Age distributions across Bud Tip and AT1 subtypes",
       x = "Age (days)", y = "Number of cells") +
  guides(fill = "none")
```

## Cell Type Abundance as % of Total Cells Per Sample

```{r composition-pct-at2}
make_pct_plot <- function(celltype_use, groups_use = c("fetal","pediatric")) {
  plot_df <- meta_df %>%
    filter(group %in% groups_use, cell_type3 == celltype_use) %>%
    group_by(batch, group, age_days) %>%
    summarise(celltype_cells = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    left_join(total_df, by = "batch") %>%
    mutate(pct = 100 * celltype_cells / total_cells) %>%
    filter(!is.na(age_days), !is.na(pct)) %>%
    arrange(group, age_days) %>%
    mutate(sample_age = paste0(batch, " (", age_days, ")")) %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    mutate(sample_age = factor(sample_age, levels = unique(sample_age))) %>%
    ungroup()

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = sample_age, y = pct, fill = group)) +
    geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.85) +
    facet_grid(. ~ group, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("fetal" = "#6A3D9A", "pediatric" = "#F28E2B")) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste0(celltype_use, " as % of total cells per sample"),
```
